response.justification: Aisha brings genuine geospatial experience through the GISCollective platform and Green Map work, and has demonstrated comfort with distributed, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby. However, the role's primary technical requirement is production-grade C++ (or Rust), and the candidate's C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago plus a bachelor thesis project. Their current and recent commercial work is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js. There is no Rust experience, no routing or navigation pipeline work, and no evidence of geospatial work at the scale or latency sensitivity that production routing tiles demand.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity: founded and operates GISCollective, built Green Map GIS apps, and gave a FOSS4G talk — signals genuine interest and hands-on experience in geo-data.
High-throughput distributed systems: at Dunnhumby handled millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency techniques in a microservices environment.
Breadth across the stack: embedded, mobile, backend, DevOps — demonstrates adaptability and willingness to work outside a comfort zone, which the JD explicitly values.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible for a senior role: only 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project; no commercial C++ work in the last 10+ years.
No Rust experience whatsoever, closing off the only acceptable alternative to C++.
GIS work is NGO/hobbyist scale with no evidence of routing tiles, map data transformation pipelines, or navigation guidance systems — the specific domain the role requires.
